Brittany washes her hair every other day, which means she uses shampoo 183 times in a leap year consisting of 366 days. Since she uses 1/4 ounce per wash, her total consumption for the year is 45.75 ounces. To determine how many 14-ounce bottles she needs, we divide the total ounces by the bottle size, resulting in approximately 3.27 bottles, so she must purchase 4 bottles to ensure she does not run out before the year ends. You can order these supplies by visiting [store_name] or calling [phone_number] to place your request.

Once the bottles are purchased, Brittany will have enough product to last through the entire leap year with a small amount left over. It is important to track usage carefully, especially since the frequency of washing remains constant regardless of the season. If she decides to change her routine or if the [location] experiences unusual weather that affects her hair care habits, she might need to adjust her calculations accordingly. Keeping an extra bottle on hand at [address] would be a wise precaution against any unexpected delays in restocking.
